spec.description: Architecture reference and replication checklist for KtransToGrafana → Grafana Cloud. Audience: engineers deploying the same setup at another datacenter.

options.content:
## Ktranslate → Grafana Cloud: architecture & datacenter replication

This guide documents the **reference architecture** used to deliver SNMP, flow, and related network telemetry into **Grafana Cloud** via [KtransToGrafana](https://github.com/Mesverrum/KtransToGrafana).

**Audience:** Commvault network / platform engineers standing up the **same capability in another datacenter** (new Docker host, new CIDRs/credentials, same Grafana Cloud stack or a new stack).

**What you get when it works:**
- SNMP metrics as `kentik_snmp_*` (device health, interfaces, sensors, etc.)
- Rolled-up flow metrics as `network_io_by_flow_*` (OTEL semantic convention names)
- ICMP reachability as `kentik_ping_*`
- Dashboards in this folder for fleet summary, per-device drilldown, and flow analysis

> This repo is a community example (not maintained by Kentik or Grafana). Use it as the deployment blueprint; open issues/PRs on GitHub for config questions.

## End-to-end data path

![Ktranslate architecture diagram](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Mesverrum/KtransToGrafana/main/ktrans_architecture.png)

![Ktranslate to Alloy OTLP path](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Mesverrum/KtransToGrafana/main/ktrans_to_alloy.png)

```
Network devices / exporters          Docker host (KtransToGrafana)              Grafana Cloud
─────────────────────────────        ─────────────────────────────            ───────────────
SNMP (161/udp)  ───────────────►  ktranslate_snmp  ──┐
NetFlow/sFlow   ───────────────►  ktranslate_flow  ──┼──►  Grafana Alloy  ──OTLP/HTTP──►  Mimir (Prometheus)
Syslog (opt.)   ───────────────►  ktranslate_syslog ─┘      (config.alloy)              Explore + dashboards
host-sflow (opt.) ─────────────►  ktranslate_flow
```

**Key idea:** ktranslate converts SNMP/flow/syslog into **OTLP metrics**; a minimal **Alloy** agent forwards them to your Grafana Cloud OTLP gateway. No full observability stack is required on the collector host.

## Containers in [compose.yaml](https://github.com/Mesverrum/KtransToGrafana/blob/main/compose.yaml)

| Service | Role | Listens / notes |
|---|---|---|
| **ktranslate_snmp** | SNMP discovery + polling via `snmp.yaml` | Updates device list from Kentik [snmp-profiles](https://github.com/kentik/snmp-profiles); emits `kentik_snmp_*` |
| **ktranslate_flow** | NetFlow/sFlow/IPFIX aggregation | UDP 9995; rollups `--rollup_top_k=100`, `--rollup_interval=60` cap cardinality |
| **ktranslate_syslog** | Syslog ingestion (optional) | UDP/TCP 1514 |
| **alloy** | OTLP receiver → Grafana Cloud exporter | Reads `GC_OTLP_URL`, `GC_OTLP_ACCOUNT`, `GC_OTLP_KEY` from `.env` |
| **host-sflow** | Demo flow from Docker host NIC (optional) | Remove if you already export flow from routers |

**Alloy preprocessing (flow only):** renames rollup metrics to `network.io.by_flow` semantic names and sets `service.name=integrations/ktranslate-netflow` so official NetFlow dashboards can be used. SNMP/ping keep distinct `OTEL_SERVICE_NAME` values so they are not rewritten.

## Which Git branch to clone?

| Branch | When to use |
|---|---|
| **main** | Single credential set, CIDR discovery — fastest PoC |
| **multicontainer_example** | Multiple SNMP credential groups (vendor/site) — one poller per group |
| **multicontainer_netbox** | Device inventory from NetBox tags/roles instead of CIDR scan |

For a **new datacenter**, choose based on how you manage credentials and inventory:

- One SNMP community / v3 profile for the site → **main**
- Different communities per vendor or zone → **multicontainer_example**
- NetBox is source of truth → **multicontainer_netbox**

Clone: `git clone https://github.com/Mesverrum/KtransToGrafana.git`

## Metric families (Prometheus / Mimir)

| Signal | Example metrics | Primary labels |
|---|---|---|
| SNMP | `kentik_snmp_CPU`, `kentik_snmp_if_OperStatus`, `kentik_snmp_entSensorValue` | `device_name`, `provider`, `if_interface_name` |
| Ping | `kentik_ping_PacketLossPct`, `kentik_ping_AvgRttMs` | `device_name` |
| Flow (rolled up) | `network_io_by_flow_bytes` | `device_name`, `network_local_address`, `network_peer_address`, `network_protocol_name` |

**Cardinality rule of thumb:** ~1,000 active series per network device (simple gear less, core/LB more). Flow is capped by rollup top-K (see repo README for active-series math).

**Do not hardcode device names in dashboards** — use the `$instance` / `$device_name` template variables (see dashboards in this folder).

## Grafana dashboards in this folder

| Dashboard | UID | Use |
|---|---|---|
| [00. Network Device Summary](/d/mavgvqv/00-network-device-summary) | `mavgvqv` | Fleet health — all SNMP devices |
| [01. Network Device Details](/d/magz6qw1/01-network-device-details) | `magz6qw1` | Per-device drilldown (tabs: Overview, Interfaces, Sensors, Connections) |
| [02. Network Flow Summary](/d/be8hpir89dds0a/02-network-flow-summary) | `be8hpir89dds0a` | Top talkers, protocols, geo — rolled-up flow |

**Copying dashboards to another Grafana stack:** use **Dashboard settings → JSON Model → Save to file** (or **Share → Export**) on each dashboard above. The [KtransToGrafana repo](https://github.com/Mesverrum/KtransToGrafana/tree/main/dashboards) also ships starter dashboard JSON you can import via **Dashboards → New → Import**.

## Stand up another site (copy this list)

### 1. Collector host
- [ ] Ubuntu Linux or WSL2 host with Docker + Compose (`docker run hello-world`)
- [ ] UDP/TCP reachability from host to SNMP (161) and flow exporters (typically 9995/udp)
- [ ] Outbound HTTPS to Grafana Cloud OTLP gateway

### 2. Clone & configure [KtransToGrafana](https://github.com/Mesverrum/KtransToGrafana)
- [ ] `git clone` the branch that matches your credential/inventory model
- [ ] `cp .env.sample .env` — set `GC_OTLP_URL`, `GC_OTLP_ACCOUNT`, `GC_OTLP_KEY` from Grafana Cloud → **Connections → OpenTelemetry**
- [ ] `cp snmp.yaml.sample snmp.yaml` — set discovery CIDRs (`/32` for single IPs) and SNMP v2/v3 credentials
- [ ] `cp config.alloy.sample config.alloy` (usually no edits unless customizing Alloy)
- [ ] `sudo chown 1000:1000 snmp.yaml` (ktranslate mutates this file during discovery)
- [ ] Set `HOST_NET` for host-sflow or remove that service if unused (`make detect-net`)

### 3. Start & validate collector
- [ ] `make preflight` then `make up` (or `docker compose up -d`)
- [ ] `make logs` — confirm SNMP discovery maps devices to Kentik profiles
- [ ] From host: `snmpwalk` to one device if discovery is empty (see repo `troubleshooting/`)

### 4. Validate Grafana Cloud
- [ ] Explore → Prometheus: `count by (device_name) (kentik_snmp_DeviceMetrics)` — one row per polled device
- [ ] Explore → Prometheus: `sum by (device_name) (rate(network_io_by_flow_bytes[5m]))` — flow if exporters configured
- [ ] Open dashboards in this folder; confirm `$datasource` and device variables populate

### 5. Import dashboards to a new stack (if not sharing this stack)
- [ ] Export JSON from the dashboards in this folder (settings → JSON Model, or Share → Export)
- [ ] Or import starter JSON from the [KtransToGrafana `dashboards/` folder](https://github.com/Mesverrum/KtransToGrafana/tree/main/dashboards)
- [ ] **Dashboards → New → Import** on the target stack; set the Prometheus datasource on import
- [ ] Re-link drilldown URLs if dashboard UIDs change after import

### 6. Production hardening (recommended)
- [ ] Secrets in `.env` — restrict file permissions; use separate `.env` per environment
- [ ] Document which CIDRs and credentials belong to this datacenter
- [ ] Tune flow `--rollup_top_k` if cardinality or cost is high
- [ ] Add syslog/ICMP only if required (each adds series)

## PromQL quick checks (Explore → Prometheus)

**SNMP devices polling:**
```promql
count by (device_name) (kentik_snmp_DeviceMetrics)
```

**SNMP active series by device (rough footprint):**
```promql
count by (device_name) ({__name__=~"kentik_snmp_.+"})
```

**Flow bytes rate by exporter:**
```promql
sum by (device_name) (rate(network_io_by_flow_bytes[5m]))
```

**Ping loss (if enabled):**
```promql
avg by (device_name) (kentik_ping_PacketLossPct)
```

If SNMP query returns rows but dashboards are empty, check the **device template variable** and datasource selector at the top of each dashboard.

## When something breaks

| Symptom | First place to look |
|---|---|
| No SNMP devices | `docker compose logs ktranslate_snmp`, `https://github.com/Mesverrum/KtransToGrafana/tree/main/troubleshooting` |
| Devices discovered but no metrics | Kentik profile match — check SysObjectID in logs vs [snmp-profiles](https://github.com/kentik/snmp-profiles) |
| No flow | Exporter IP/port, firewall UDP 9995, rollup logs on `ktranslate_flow` |
| Metrics in Cloud but wrong names | Alloy transform — SNMP should **not** get `integrations/ktranslate-netflow` service name |
| High cardinality / cost | Lower `--rollup_top_k`; review label cardinality on `network_io_by_flow_*` |

**References**
- [KtransToGrafana README](https://github.com/Mesverrum/KtransToGrafana#readme)
- [Kentik ktranslate](https://github.com/kentik/ktranslate)
- [Kentik SNMP profiles](https://github.com/kentik/snmp-profiles)
- [Grafana Cloud OTLP](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/send-data/otlp/)
- [Advanced Ktranslate Configuration](https://github.com/kentik/ktranslate/wiki/Advanced-Ktranslate-Configuration) — detailed ktranslate config options

**Contact:** [KtransToGrafana Issues](https://github.com/Mesverrum/KtransToGrafana/issues) on GitHub.
